The Research
Peptide Guide

Complete guide: categories, protocols, dosing, reconstitution, stacks, and storage. 19 peptides with reference data.

Jump to guide

FRAMEWORK

The 3-Level Framework

Peptides are precision tools — they work best on a solid health foundation.

01

Foundational Health

  • •Sleep quality
  • •Strength training & movement
  • •Adequate protein intake
  • •Stable blood sugar
  • •Stress management
02

Hormone Optimization

  • •Estrogen, progesterone
  • •Testosterone
  • •Thyroid hormones
  • •Metabolic balance
03

Precision Tools (Peptides)

  • •Foundational peptides (NAD+, GHK-Cu)
  • •Seasonal peptides (BPC-157, CJC-1295)
  • •Situational peptides (Selank, Melanotan)

Important

Human Research vs Animal Research

Clinical Trials (Humans)

Some peptides (tirzepatide, tesamorelin, retatrutide) have undergone or are undergoing human clinical trials. They have an established safety profile.

Retatrutide (Phase 3)BPC-157 (Pilot 2025)

Preclinical Studies (Animals)

Most research peptides (MOTS-c, TB-500, Selank, SS-31) have data primarily from animal model studies. Animal results do not always translate to humans.

MOTS-cTB-500SS-31Selank

All peptides in our catalog are intended exclusively for research and laboratory use. Not medical advice.

Cheat Sheet

Quick Reference: Reconstitution

5 mg + 2 mL → 2.5 mg/mL

250 mcg = 0.1 mL = 10 units

10 mg + 2 mL → 5.0 mg/mL

250 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units

50 mg + 3 mL → 16.7 mg/mL

1.7 mg ≈ 0.10 mL ≈ 10 units

General rule

Concentration = amount (mg) ÷ water volume (mL)

1 mL = 100 units on a U-100 insulin syringe

Order research peptides

>98% HPLC purity, fast InPost delivery, secure Stripe payments.
